people.workExperience: Assistant Professor,
people.researchInterests: My research focus includes study of fluid mechanics and combustion processes using advanced optical diagnostic techniques at practically relevant thermo-fluidic conditions. Below are the broad areas of my research: Advanced laser diagnostics: Spectroscopic techniques such as Rayleigh/Raman scattering, filtered Rayleigh scattering for multi-scalar field measurements, gas-phase spectroscopy and non-linear diagnostics. Propulsion: Air-breathing and rocket propulsion, cryogenic combustion, micro-thrusters based on green propellants. Power generation: Power generation gas turbine combustor, Oxy-Coal combustion, Circulating fluidized bed combustors. Fire Safety Research: Fire dynamics, radiative heat transfer, soot-chemistry, fire suppression technology, safety codes and standards for high energy density fuel and propellant.

people.name: Srisha Rao M. V.
people.education: PhD (Aerospace) - Indian Institute of Science, Bengaluru [2014] ME (Aerospace) - Indian Institute of Science, Bengaluru [2008] BE (Mechanical) - National Institue of Technology Karnataka, Surathkal [2006]
people.workExperience: Post-Doctoral Research Scholar - Muroran Institute of Technology, Japan [2014-2016]
people.researchInterests: Exciting research is being carried out in both external and internal gasdynamic phenomena at high Mach numbers. Dominantly experimental tools are employed to study the phenomena including high speed visualization and optical flow diagnostics such as PIV and PLIF. Several aspects of the unsteady non-linear nature of shock-shear layer interactions are being explored. Some of the interesting problems are supersonic mixing and its enhancement, complex nozzles, shock-mixing layer interactions, flow control in high speed flows, development of ground-based high speed aerodynamic test facilities.

people.name: S. Saravanan
people.education: B.Tech (Aeronautical Engineering) M.I.T. Anna University, Chennai, 1995. M.Tech (Aerospace Engineering) Indian Institute of Technology, Kanpur, 1997. Ph. D in Aerospace Engineering at Indian Institute of Science, Bangalore, 2007.
people.workExperience: Spent six months as a Post Graduate Trainee after completing his M.Tech in the Experimental Aerodynamics Division at National Aerospace Laboratory, Bangalore. Scientific Officer Gr.II Feb. 1998 - 2003 Scientific Officer Gr.I 2003 - 2006 Senior Scientific Officer 2006 - 2012 Principal Research Scientist 2012 - Till Date
people.researchInterests: Design and develpement of an accelerometer force balance (i.e., 3 and 6-component accelerometer balances) to measure the aerodynamic forces and moments for various aerodynamic configuration within test duration of 1 ms. Aerospikes ahead of the blunt body, concentrated energy deposition along the stagnation streamline and forward facing jet in the stagnation zone of a blunt body are investigated for keeping the heat load to acceptable levels. Direct measurement of skin friction on backward facing step (2 mm step height) and on double-wedge model exposed to laminar hypersonic flow, using the PZT-5A piezo-ceramic gauge. Effect of permanent magnetic field on the aerodynamic drag coefficient (Cd) and heat transfer rates for a 120o apex angle blunt cone configuration at Mach 8 in the IISc free piston driven hypersonic shock tunnel. Using anodized aluminum pressure-sensitive paint (AA-PSP) on the truncated cone in the high supersonic tunnel of the University of Manchester at Mach 5 Shock tunnel testing of scaled configurations, Instrumentation and experimental gas dynamics.
